GT LEARN #239 🪟 Sliding Window Maximum HARD Deque / Array
exp: [3,3,5,5,6,7]

📝 Problem Statement

Given an array nums and a sliding window of size k, return the maximum value in each window as it slides from left to right.

🧠 Solving Approach

Use a Deque (Double Ended Queue) to store indices. The deque maintains indices of useful elements in decreasing order of their values. The front of the deque always holds the index of the current window's maximum.

Maintain Decreasing Order Remove smaller elements from back before adding new one.
Remove Expired If front index is out of window range, remove it.
Record Max Once window size is reached, record nums[deque[0]].
Time: O(N) Space: O(K)

🧪 Test Cases (click to load)

T1 Standard exp:[3,3,5,5,6,7]
[1,3,-1,-3,5,3,6,7] k=3
T2 Ascending exp:[3,4,5]
[1,2,3,4,5] k=3
T3 Single exp:[1]
[1] k=1
—
📜 Python Code
📊 Visualization — Deque (Indices/Values)
📊 Array + Sliding Window
In Window Current i Max in Window
🪟 Current Window (size k)
📉 Deque (q) — q[0] is always the current max
Empty
FRONT (q[0]) = Current Maximum ⟶ Body (decreasing candidates) ⟶ BACK (newest)
🔢 nums[q[]] — Values in Deque Order
Output (Max of each window)
0/0 INIT
←→ 100%
